name: aidl
title: aidl
description:
Binder generator of AIDL interfaces
aidl is a utility to generate Java and/or C++ code from Android's
Android Interface Definition Language (AIDL). AIDL is similar to
other IDLs you might have worked with. It allows you to define the
programming interface that both the client and service agree upon in
order to communicate with each other using interprocess communication
(IPC). On Android, one process cannot normally access the memory of
another process. So to talk, they need to decompose their objects
into primitives that the operating system can understand, and
marshall the objects across that boundary for you. The code to do
that marshalling is tedious to write, so Android handles it for you
with AIDL. This package provides two tools:
.
* "aidl" which generates Java bindings.
* "aidl-cpp" which generates C++ bindings.
.
For more info, see:
https://developer.android.com/guide/components/aidl.html

commands.name: aidl
commands.description: Java bindings generator of AIDL interfaces

arguments.description: Generate dependency file next to the output file with the name based on the input file.
arguments.raw: -a  Generate dependency file next to the output file with the name based on the input file.

arguments.description: Fail when trying to compile a parcelable.
arguments.raw: -b  Fail when trying to compile a parcelable.

arguments.description: Generate dependency file in a format ninja understands.
arguments.raw: -ninja  Generate dependency file in a format ninja understands.

commands.name: aidl-cpp
commands.description: C++ bindings generator of AIDL interfaces

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use aidl because Binder generator of AIDL interfaces.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use aidl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aidl OPTIONS INPUT [OUTPUT]` as documented upstream for aidl.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aidl --preprocess OUTPUT INPUT...` as documented upstream for aidl.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair aidl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `aidl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: aidl - Java bindings generator of AIDL interfaces
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install android-platform-system-tools-aidl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `aidl --preprocess OUTPUT INPUT...`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Binder generator of AIDL interfaces.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: aidl - Java bindings generator of AIDL interfaces.
